prompt:
weird issue: every time we land a commit on main, our `deploy-staging` job runs **twice**, back to back, with the same SHA. it depends on two upstream jobs (`unit` and `integration`) that both pass. when both of them finish, deploy-staging fires twice. occasional CI flake means we sometimes get *three* runs.

relevant chunk:

```yaml
  - name: deploy-staging
    plan:
      - in_parallel:
          - get: app-image
            trigger: true
            passed: [unit]
          - get: source
            trigger: true
            passed: [integration]
      - task: deploy
        file: source/ci/deploy.yml
```

why is it double-running and how do we fix it without losing the "deploy on every successful main commit" behavior? we still want the deploy to be triggered automatically.

assertions.name: diagnoses two triggers as cause
assertions.description: Response identifies that two `trigger: true` gets cause two independent triggers, hence two runs

assertions.name: fix uses single trigger
assertions.description: Final YAML has `trigger: true` on EXACTLY ONE get (not both)

assertions.name: fix mirrors passed lists
assertions.description: Final YAML lists BOTH `unit` AND `integration` in `passed:` on BOTH gets (locks to the same end-to-end version)

assertions.name: calls out version skew risk
assertions.description: Response notes that the original split `passed:` lists also risk fetching mismatched versions of `app-image` and `source`

assertions.name: preserves auto-deploy
assertions.description: Response confirms (or shows) that the fix still auto-deploys on every successful main commit — not converted to manual

assertions.name: valid yaml
assertions.description: Output YAML is syntactically valid Concourse v8 syntax (correct indentation, valid keys)
